name: Melitzanosalata Recipe (Greek Eggplant Dip)
method: Hold the eggplant whole and penetrate it in a few locations with a
spoon.
Get the eggplant smoked.
Position the eggplant over a gas burner, barbecue, or under a broiler,
and roast until the skin is completely fried and the eggplant is very
tender, flipping it around with a pair of tweezers.
Cool the eggplant and remove it. Put the eggplant in a pan and put it
aside until it is cold enough to treat.
Slice off the burnt skin and discard it. Break the eggplant into pieces
and put it in a colander to get out of any extra juices leftover.
To a mixing cup, pass the eggplant. Add the cabbage, coriander, lime
juice, olive oil, and garlic.
Transfer salt and black pepper and seasoning. Mix to blend and divide
the eggplant into smaller pieces with your fork.
At this stage, it is a smart idea to protect and cold the eggplant
dipping in the refrigerator for a few short minutes if you have the
opportunity.
To a serving dish, pass the eggplant dipping and scatter.
Toss in the olive oil. Garnish with lime zest, coriander, sweet
onions, olives, and a feta sprinkle.
Use crumbly bread or flatbread to serve.
